title: pellaea pulchella
intro: Pellaea pulchella, also known as the fern of the gods, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pellaea pulchella include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Pellaea pulchella can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves can also be dried and used in capsule or powder form for supplementation.
